questions.question: What is a tree data structure?
options: A) A collection of linked lists
B) A hierarchical arrangement of nodes
C) A data structure used for graph traversal
D) A type of array
questions.explanation: A tree data structure is a hierarchical arrangement of nodes, where each node has a value and zero or more child nodes.

questions.question: What is a binary tree?
options: A) A tree with only two child nodes
B) A tree with at least two child nodes
C) A tree with no child nodes
D) A tree with only one child node
questions.explanation: A binary tree is a tree in which each node has at most two child nodes.

questions.question: What is the purpose of tree traversals?
options: A) To insert new nodes
B) To delete existing nodes
C) To visit each node in a specific order
D) To search for a specific node
questions.explanation: The purpose of tree traversals is to visit each node in a specific order, such as pre-order, in-order, or post-order.

questions.question: What is a threaded binary tree?
options: A) A binary tree with additional pointers
B) A binary tree without additional pointers
C) A tree data structure that uses linked lists
D) A tree data structure that uses arrays
questions.explanation: A threaded binary tree is a binary tree with additional pointers, which allow for efficient insertion and deletion of nodes.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of traversing a binary tree using in-order traversal?
options: A) O(n)
B) O(n log n)
C) O(n log n) in the worst case
D) O(log n)
questions.explanation: The time complexity of traversing a binary tree using in-order traversal is O(n), where n is the number of nodes in the tree.
